title: Malvastrum macrostachyum
taxonomy: Malvastrum macrostachyum belongs to the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family, which comprises around 200 genera of flowering plants. This family is part of the larger order Malvales. Malvastrum macrostachyum is a species within the genus Malvastrum, which is native to the Americas. The genus Malvastrum is often classified as part of the tribe Malvinae. Malvastrum macrostachyum is further classified into the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Malvastrum macrostachyum, a plant species native to South America, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves, roots, and stems are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
